title: Week 7 PPTX: Adding and Manipulating the Data
summary: Source slide deck for Week 7 covering INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, COMMIT, AUTO_INCREMENT, and data manipulation examples.

Slide 5
Inserting Data Statement
The MySQL INSERT INTO Statement
The INSERT INTO statement is used to insert new records in a table.
INSERT INTO Syntax
It is possible to write the INSERT INTO statement in two ways:
Specify both the column names and the values to be inserted:
2. If you are adding values for all the columns of the table you do not need to specify the column names in the SQL query. However, make sure the order of the values is in the same order as the columns in the table

Slide 11
(B) The special concept of NULL
11

Slide 12
MySQL NULL Values
What is a NULL Value? 🡪 A field with a NULL value is a field whose value:
can be omitted during record INSERT, UPDATE (assigned a special NULL value),
but can be specified at any time
IS NULL Syntax and IS NOT NULL Syntax
12

Slide 13
(C) Bulk Uploading Data
13

Slide 14
Bulk Uploading Data in MySQL
Bulk loading means to create and insert large numbers of rows (Import Data) into a MySQL table.
Use a combination of these statements:
Create Database
Create Tables
Insert Data (usually many of these)
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=OnXB3ZRrOW0

Slide 16
(D) Creating Dummy Data
16

Slide 17
Creating Dummy Data
Dummy data is mock data generated at random as a substitute for live data in testing environments. In other words, dummy data acts as a placeholder for live data, the latter of which testers only introduce once it’s determined that the trail program does not have any unintended, negative impact on the underlying data
Take your pick from a number of online options
https://www.mockaroo.com/
https://analystanswers.com/dummy-data-definition-example-how-to-generate-it/
